member.name: Phil Brickell
member.synopsis: Phil Brickell is the Labour MP for Bolton West , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Money Laundering: International Cooperation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many mutual legal assistance requests received by the UK Central Authority related to money laundering for each year from 2018 to 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office is the central authority for Mutual Legal Assistance (MLA) for England, Wales and Northern Ireland except for tax and fiscal customs criminal matters, which are coordinated by HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC). Requests seeking assistance so...

writtenQuestions.heading: National Crime Agency: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what was the (a) annual budget, broken down by core budget and external funding lines and (b) headcount FTE in each of the past 5 years for the National Crime Agency’s (i) National Economic Crime Comm
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a matter of longstanding policy, the National Crime Agency does not provide breakdowns and estimates on the specific budget allocations to target particular crime types. Details about the Agency’s budgets can be found online in our Annual Report and Ac...

writtenQuestions.heading: National Crime Agency: Corruption and Economic Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate she has made of the percentage of the National Crime Agency’s core annual budget for each of the last five years that was spent on tackling economic crime and corruption.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a matter of longstanding policy, the National Crime Agency does not provide breakdowns and estimates on the specific budget allocations to target particular crime types. Details about the Agency’s budgets can be found online in our Annual Report and Ac...

writtenQuestions.heading: Corruption
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what the annual budget for the UK Action Against Corruption programme is for (a) 2024-25, and (b) 2025-26.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The spend for the UK Action Against Corruption programme was £6,524,964 in 2024-25 and £6,739,378 in 2025-26.

writtenQuestions.heading: Developing Countries: Corruption
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, when the next 5-year business case for the UK Action to Support Developing Countries Fighting Corruption programme will be published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The business case for the current UK Action Against Corruption programme, covering 2025 to 2030, is available on Dev Tracker at the following link - https://iati.fcdo.gov.uk/iati_documents/S40031011.odt

writtenQuestions.heading: Developing Countries: Corruption
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, when the annual review of the UK Action to Support Developing Countries Fighting Corruption programme for the 2024/25 financial year will be published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The relevant report has been prepared for publication and should be available on the Development Tracker website shortly.

writtenQuestions.heading: Fraud Investigation Service: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what percentage of the annual budget for HMRC’s Fraud Investigation Service was allocated to its Proceeds of Crime division in each of the previous 5 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon member to the answer on 17 April 2026 to UIN124027 Fraud Investigation Service

writtenQuestions.heading: Fraud Investigation Service: Finance and Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what was the annual a) budget, and b) headcount FTE for HMRC’s Fraud Investigation Service for each of the years 2024/25 and 2025/26.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon member to the answer on 17 April 2026 to UIN124027 Fraud Investigation Service

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services: Greater Manchester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, a) what assessment has the Department made of orthodontic waiting times in the Greater Manchester region and b) whether the Department is taking steps to bring down those waiting times.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on the average waiting times for National Health Service orthodontic treatment is not held centrally.Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for commissioning primary care services, including NHS dentistry and orthodontic services, to meet the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Disclosure of Information
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what the estimated timeframe is of his Department's review of whistleblowing policy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government has announced its intention to launch its whistleblowing consultation in the summer but has not yet decided on an exact publication date. Details will be published on GOV.UK in due course.

writtenQuestions.heading: Disclosure of Information
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, when his Department plans to start its review of whistleblowing policy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government has announced its intention to launch its whistleblowing consultation in the summer but has not yet decided on an exact publication date. Details will be published on GOV.UK in due course.

writtenQuestions.heading: Democracy: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Wales, what steps she is taking with Cabinet colleagues to help protect Welsh democracy from foreign interference.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is fully committed to upholding and strengthening our democracy by protecting against foreign interference, improving political transparency, adding tougher checks for donations and closing loopholes by reinforcing electoral legislation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Ambulance Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will take steps with NHS Ambulance Trusts to assess the potential merits of enabling humanitarian charities to purchase end-of-life ambulances directly.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England does not collect or hold information centrally on numbers of decommissioned National Health Service ambulances donated or sold to humanitarian charities.Decommissioned NHS ambulances are generally sold at auction, mainly in the United Kingdom....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Ambulance Services: Ukraine

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
